name: OLED 0.96"
tagline: Crisp 128×64 pixel OLED display over I2C — no backlight, ultra low power
description: The 0.96" OLED module uses an SSD1306 driver and communicates over I2C with just 2 wires (SDA + SCL). It draws only ~20mA at full brightness and has excellent contrast — each pixel is self-illuminating. Perfect for project dashboards, sensor readouts, menus, and animations. Works at both 3.3V and 5V.

specs.colors: Monochrome (white, blue, or yellow-blue depending on variant)

pinout.name: SCL
pinout.desc: I2C Clock → Arduino A5 (UNO) / D22 (Mega) / GPIO22 (ESP32)

pinout.name: SDA
pinout.desc: I2C Data → Arduino A4 (UNO) / D21 (Mega) / GPIO21 (ESP32)

wiringNote: Default I2C address is 0x3C. If two OLEDs on same bus, solder the SA0 jumper on one module to change its address to 0x3D.
libraryInstall: Library Manager → Install 'Adafruit SSD1306' (it will prompt to also install Adafruit GFX — click yes)
commonMistakes: Wrong I2C address (try 0x3D if 0x3C doesn't work)
Not calling display.display() after drawing — nothing shows until you call this
Forgetting to install Adafruit GFX dependency
Running i2C scan if unsure of address: use i2c_scanner sketch
